name: Security State Bank Of Marine
description_short: Community bank established in 1919 offering checking, savings, mortgages, business loans, and digital banking across four Minnesota locations.
description_long:
Security State Bank of Marine is a full-service community bank founded in May 1919, operating for over a century in Minnesota. The bank maintains four branch locations in Marine on St. Croix, Scandia, Chisago Lakes, and Forest Lake, positioning itself as a local financial institution with deep community roots. The bank's longevity and regional presence reflect its positioning as a stable, established financial provider rather than a newer fintech or online-only bank.

The bank offers comprehensive personal and business banking services across multiple product categories. Personal banking includes checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, mortgages, auto loans, and specialized accounts like Piggy Bank Savers for children. Business customers can access small business checking, Marine Business Checking accounts, and business loans. The bank also provides insurance products including auto, home, business, life, and health & Medicare coverage through partnerships. Digital services include mobile deposit capture, bill pay, online statements, and Card Valet security features, with a digital banking platform upgrade planned for mid-March 2025.

Security State Bank distinguishes itself through its century-long operational history and multi-branch community presence in the Twin Cities metro area. The bank emphasizes personalized service with loan professionals available for individual consultations on mortgages, auto loans, and business financing. Their mobile and digital offerings—including Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Samsung Pay integration—demonstrate modernization efforts while maintaining traditional banking services. The planned digital banking platform upgrade suggests ongoing investment in technology and user experience.

As a traditional community bank, Security State Bank of Marine serves customers seeking established local banking relationships with in-person branch access. The institution is best suited for individuals and small businesses in its service area who value community connection alongside modern digital banking conveniences. Customers outside the four-branch service area would need to verify accessibility, and those seeking specialized products like credit cards or investment services may need to evaluate offerings thoroughly. The bank represents a middle-ground option between large national banks and online-only financial institutions.
services: Savings accounts and certificates of deposit
Small business checking and business loans
Mobile deposit capture via smartphone app
Online bill pay with autopay and payment reminders
Card Valet security and alert management
Mobile wallet integration (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay)
Auto, home, business, life, and health insurance products
pros: Four physical branch locations in Twin Cities area providing in-person service and local community presence
Over 105 years of continuous operation since 1919 demonstrates stability and established reputation
24/7 digital banking with mobile deposit capture, bill pay, and online statements for convenience
Mobile wallet support including Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Samsung Pay for contactless payments
Card Valet security features with location-based alerts and usage controls for debit and credit cards
Specialized accounts for different life stages including Piggy Bank Savers for children
Loan professionals available for personalized consultation on mortgages, auto, and business loans
cons: Limited geographic service area with only four branches in Minnesota, not accessible to distant customers
Website lacks detailed information on interest rates, fees, minimum balances, and loan terms
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or advisory services compared to larger banks
Digital banking platform upgrade scheduled for mid-March 2025 may create temporary disruption or unavailability
Limited transparency on insurance product details and whether these are direct offerings or partnerships

contact.address: 120 Judd St, Marine On St. Croix, MN 55047

best_for: Residents and businesses in Marine on St. Croix, Scandia, Chisago Lakes, and Forest Lake seeking community banking with personal service
Small business owners in the Twin Cities metro area needing local loan professionals and business checking accounts
Families wanting specialized children's savings accounts combined with full-service banking convenience
Customers prioritizing established bank stability and local presence over online-only banking options
diagnosis: Security State Bank of Marine is best for individuals and small business owners in its four-branch Minnesota service area seeking traditional community banking with established stability and modern digital conveniences. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—customers outside the Twin Cities metro area cannot easily access branches, and full product details regarding rates, fees, and terms remain unavailable on the website, requiring direct contact for accurate comparison.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 120 Judd St, Marine On St. Croix, MN 55047
